reasoning: NATO Article 5 has only been invoked once in history (following the September 11 attacks). The historical base rate for its invocation in any given year is therefore extremely low (around 1.3%). For the roughly 7-month period specified in the question (May 28 to December 31, 2025), the base rate probability is less than 1%. Although geopolitical tensions involving NATO members (such as the war in Ukraine or instability in the Middle East) slightly elevate the risk of border incidents, NATO members have consistently demonstrated a strong preference for de-escalation rather than invoking Article 5 over isolated incidents (e.g., the 2022 Poland missile incident). Because I am constrained from searching for specific events that may have occurred in late 2025, I must rely heavily on the base rate. The systemic incentives to avoid direct major-power conflict mean the true probability almost certainly rests at or below the lower bound of 0.02.
